findings.description: The URL in the PDF for the administrative record is not rendered as a clickable hyperlink in the HTML, and the email address for the project manager is also not linked. However, no fabricated links are present.
findings.evidence: PDF contains URL 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche' and email 'caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov' — both are present in the HTML as plain text but not as hyperlinks.

findings.description: On page 2, the contact email for Caroline Bari is listed as 'caroline.bari@deq.nc.gov' in the PDF, but on page 1 it appears as 'caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov'. The HTML preserves both instances accurately, so no error in conversion, but this is a discrepancy within the source document itself.
findings.evidence: Page 1 PDF: 'caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov'; Page 2 PDF: 'caroline.bari@deq.nc.gov'. HTML matches both.

findings.description: The document has no true hierarchical section headings in the PDF — it is a continuous fact sheet. The HTML uses h2 for the title 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' which is reasonable. There is no h1 used as a document title, though the outer <main> wrapper exists. This is a minor structural choice.
findings.evidence: HTML uses <h2> for the title block. No h1 is present.
